mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction that forces corporate earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient supply-side output that outpaces rate hike headwinds and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 0.5 percent month-over-month during tightening yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 235.0 triggers inflation fears that compress real discount rates and force equity deleveraging, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 235.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that drives wage growth expectations and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising total employment and falling unemployment yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above 2.0 percent annualized sustains corporate revenue expansion and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent annualized is followed by negative 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 99.0 indicates a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output capacity and triggers negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production below 99.0 is followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.
